title: pleopeltis subnuda
intro: Pleopeltis subnuda, also known as the naked polypody or barefoot polypody, is a type of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pleopeltis subnuda include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Pleopeltis subnuda can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using a decoction to create a tea or tincture for medicinal use.
